name: Expansion vs New Logo Acquisition
description: Leadership debates investing in expansion revenue from existing customers versus acquiring new logos, simulating long-term revenue impact.
seed_text:
Intercom's executive team is debating the FY26 GTM investment split between customer expansion and new logo acquisition. The company needs to grow ARR from $240M to $310M (29% growth) to hit the board's target, and the question is where to allocate the incremental $8M in GTM investment.

Current state of the business:
- Net Revenue Retention (NRR): 112% — good but below best-in-class SaaS (120%+)
- Gross Revenue Retention: 91% — mid-market churn is the drag
- New logo ARR added last year: $48M from 1,200 new customers
- Expansion ARR from existing customers: $29M (seat expansion 60%, upsell to higher tiers 25%, add-on products 15%)
- CAC for new logos: $18,200 average (12-month payback)
- CAC for expansion: $4,100 average (3-month payback)
- Sales team: 85 AEs focused on new business, 12 Account Managers handling expansion for 3,400 accounts

Expansion opportunity analysis:
1. 1,800 customers on Starter/Pro tiers have usage patterns suggesting they'd benefit from Premium ($6.2M potential)
2. 940 customers haven't adopted Fin AI yet — average uplift when they do is $12K ARR ($11.3M potential)
3. 420 accounts have grown headcount 20%+ but haven't added seats ($3.8M potential)
4. Cross-sell into new departments (marketing, sales) is untapped — 0 customers use Intercom outside of support

New logo market sizing:
- TAM for AI-first customer support: $12B, Intercom has ~2% share
- Greenfield opportunity in mid-market alone: 14,000 companies matching ICP that don't use any modern support platform
- Win rates have improved from 22% to 31% since Fin launch

The simulation should model the revenue trajectory under different investment splits (70/30, 50/50, 30/70 expansion vs acquisition), predict NRR and growth rate outcomes, and identify the optimal allocation for sustainable growth.
expected_outputs: Revenue trajectory forecast per investment split scenario
NRR improvement potential from expansion investment
New logo acquisition curve at different spend levels
Optimal AE vs AM headcount ratio
